pain_point_label: Reconciliation Opacity Across Multi-Campus Claims & Transfers

matches.match_rationale: The core problem is that claim submissions, fund receipts, and rejections exist across spreadsheets, government portals, and accounting systems with inconsistent formats and identifiers.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI-assisted ETL layer that ingests government remittance advices, campus submission exports, and ledger entries, normalizes IDs and amounts, and outputs a canonical reconciliation table.

matches.match_rationale: Orphaned claims — submitted months ago with no receipt and no rejection — are anomalies relative to expected claim lifecycle patterns.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A claim aging monitor that scores each open government receivable on likelihood-of-resolution based on submission date, campus, program type, and historical patterns.

matches.match_rationale: Reconciliation is a multi-step workflow: retrieve government remittance data, match against submitted claims, split partial fund releases by campus, post matched entries, and flag unresolved items.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A reconciliation agent that runs nightly, pulls remittance files from government portals, applies matching logic across campus ledgers, auto-posts matched claims, and generates a daily exceptions report.

matches.match_rationale: The CFO cannot forecast government revenue reliably because fund receipts arrive with unpredictable timing. A time series model can generate probabilistic revenue forecasts.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A government revenue forecasting dashboard that projects expected fund arrivals by campus and program over the next 60-90 days based on historical lag distributions.

matches.match_rationale: When auditors request explanations for government revenue variances or accounts receivable aging, accounting staff must manually compose responses.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI-assisted variance explanation generator that produces a structured memo tracing the contributing claims, their submission dates, partial receipts, and outstanding balances.
